example.question: What companies have a business segment that serves developers?
example.gold_cypher:
MATCH (c:Company)-[:HAS_SEGMENT]->(s:BusinessSegment)
WHERE s.company_name = c.name
MATCH (s)-[:SERVES]->(:CustomerType {name: $customer_type})
RETURN DISTINCT c.name AS company ORDER BY company

example.question: From what we have here, what companies have a business segment that monetizes via device sales?
example.gold_cypher:
MATCH (c:Company)-[:HAS_SEGMENT]->(s:BusinessSegment)
WHERE s.company_name = c.name
MATCH (s)-[:OFFERS]->(root_revenue_1:Offering)
WHERE root_revenue_1.company_name = c.name
MATCH (root_revenue_1)-[:OFFERS*0..]->(revenue_offering_1:Offering)-[:MONETIZES_VIA]->(:RevenueModel {name: $revenue_model})
WHERE revenue_offering_1.company_name = c.name
RETURN DISTINCT c.name AS company ORDER BY company

example.question: From what we have here, show the companies whose segments together let them serve merchant businesses and SMBs.
example.gold_cypher:
MATCH (c:Company)
MATCH (c:Company)-[:HAS_SEGMENT]->(s1:BusinessSegment)
WHERE s1.company_name = c.name
MATCH (s1)-[:SERVES]->(:CustomerType {name: $customer_type_1})
MATCH (c:Company)-[:HAS_SEGMENT]->(s2:BusinessSegment)
WHERE s2.company_name = c.name
MATCH (s2)-[:SERVES]->(:CustomerType {name: $customer_type_2})
RETURN DISTINCT c.name AS company ORDER BY company

example.question: Identify business segments whose company operates in Canada and that serve developers.
example.gold_cypher:
MATCH (company:Company)-[:OPERATES_IN]->(place:Place)
WHERE any(requested_place IN $places WHERE requested_place = place.name OR requested_place IN coalesce(place.includes_places, []) OR requested_place IN coalesce(place.within_places, []))
WITH DISTINCT company
MATCH (company:Company)-[:HAS_SEGMENT]->(s:BusinessSegment)
WHERE s.company_name = company.name
MATCH (s)-[:SERVES]->(:CustomerType {name: $customer_type})
RETURN DISTINCT company.name AS company, s.name AS segment ORDER BY company, segment

example.question: Based on this graph, list up to 4 descendant offerings of Loyalty Cloud for Redwood Retail.
example.gold_cypher:
MATCH (c:Company)-[:HAS_SEGMENT]->(:BusinessSegment)-[:OFFERS]->(root:Offering)
MATCH (root)-[:OFFERS*0..]->(o:Offering)
WHERE root.name IN $offerings AND root.company_name = c.name AND o.company_name = c.name AND c.name IN $companies
RETURN DISTINCT c.name AS company, o.name AS offering ORDER BY company, offering LIMIT 4

example.question: Based on this graph, name the top 5 channels by segment usage within segments at Lattice Finance operating in United States.
example.gold_cypher:
MATCH (company:Company)-[:OPERATES_IN]->(place:Place)
WHERE any(requested_place IN $places WHERE requested_place = place.name OR requested_place IN coalesce(place.includes_places, []) OR requested_place IN coalesce(place.within_places, []))
WITH DISTINCT company
WHERE company.name IN $companies
MATCH (company)-[:HAS_SEGMENT]->(s:BusinessSegment)-[:SELLS_THROUGH]->(channel:Channel)
WHERE s.company_name = company.name
RETURN channel.name AS channel, COUNT(DISTINCT [company.name, s.name]) AS segment_count
ORDER BY segment_count DESC, channel
LIMIT $limit

example.question: In this graph, list the business segments at Redwood Retail whose company operates in United States and that serve manufacturers and sell through distributors.
example.gold_cypher:
MATCH (company:Company)-[:OPERATES_IN]->(place:Place)
WHERE any(requested_place IN $places WHERE requested_place = place.name OR requested_place IN coalesce(place.includes_places, []) OR requested_place IN coalesce(place.within_places, []))
WITH DISTINCT company
MATCH (company:Company)-[:HAS_SEGMENT]->(s:BusinessSegment)
WHERE s.company_name = company.name AND company.name IN $companies
MATCH (s)-[:SERVES]->(:CustomerType {name: $customer_type})
MATCH (s)-[:SELLS_THROUGH]->(:Channel {name: $channel})
RETURN DISTINCT company.name AS company, s.name AS segment ORDER BY company, segment

example.question: Which companies can serve IT professionals and government agencies when you look across their segments?
example.gold_cypher:
MATCH (c:Company)
MATCH (c:Company)-[:HAS_SEGMENT]->(s1:BusinessSegment)
WHERE s1.company_name = c.name
MATCH (s1)-[:SERVES]->(:CustomerType {name: $customer_type_1})
MATCH (c:Company)-[:HAS_SEGMENT]->(s2:BusinessSegment)
WHERE s2.company_name = c.name
MATCH (s2)-[:SERVES]->(:CustomerType {name: $customer_type_2})
RETURN DISTINCT c.name AS company ORDER BY company

MATCH (s)-[:SERVES]->(:CustomerType {name: $customer_type})
RETURN COUNT(DISTINCT s.name) > 0 AS is_match

example.question: Based on this graph, identify companies with offerings under Analytics Studio that use licensing.
example.gold_cypher:
MATCH (company:Company)
MATCH (company)-[:HAS_SEGMENT]->(:BusinessSegment)-[:OFFERS]->(root:Offering)
MATCH (root)-[:OFFERS*0..]->(o:Offering)
WHERE root.name IN $offerings AND root.company_name = company.name AND o.company_name = company.name
MATCH (o)-[:MONETIZES_VIA]->(:RevenueModel {name: $revenue_model})
RETURN DISTINCT company.name AS company ORDER BY company

Based on this graph, top 5 channels by segment count within segments at Aurora Systems.
example.gold_cypher:
MATCH (company:Company)
WHERE company.name IN $companies
MATCH (company)-[:HAS_SEGMENT]->(s:BusinessSegment)-[:SELLS_THROUGH]->(channel:Channel)
WHERE s.company_name = company.name
RETURN channel.name AS channel, COUNT(DISTINCT [company.name, s.name]) AS segment_count
ORDER BY segment_count DESC, channel
LIMIT $limit

ORDER BY segment_count DESC, channel
LIMIT $limit

Based on this graph, list up to 3 companies operating in Mexico.
example.gold_cypher:
MATCH (company:Company)-[:OPERATES_IN]->(place:Place)
WHERE any(requested_place IN $places WHERE requested_place = place.name OR requested_place IN coalesce(place.includes_places, []) OR requested_place IN coalesce(place.within_places, []))
RETURN DISTINCT company.name AS company ORDER BY company LIMIT 3

example.question: Based on this graph, what business segments sit at companies operating in Germany and sell through direct sales?
example.gold_cypher:
MATCH (company:Company)-[:OPERATES_IN]->(place:Place)
WHERE any(requested_place IN $places WHERE requested_place = place.name OR requested_place IN coalesce(place.includes_places, []) OR requested_place IN coalesce(place.within_places, []))
WITH DISTINCT company
MATCH (company:Company)-[:HAS_SEGMENT]->(s:BusinessSegment)
WHERE s.company_name = company.name
MATCH (s)-[:SELLS_THROUGH]->(:Channel {name: $channel})
RETURN DISTINCT company.name AS company, s.name AS segment ORDER BY company, segment

WITH DISTINCT company
MATCH (company:Company)-[:HAS_SEGMENT]->(s:BusinessSegment)
WHERE s.company_name = company.name
MATCH (s)-[:SELLS_THROUGH]->(:Channel {name: $channel})
RETURN DISTINCT company.name AS company, s.name AS segment ORDER BY company, segment

In this graph, list up to 4 offerings in the Consumer Commerce segment of Redwood Retail.

WHERE s.company_name = c.name AND o.company_name = c.name AND s.name IN $segments AND c.name IN $companies
RETURN DISTINCT c.name AS company, s.name AS segment, o.name AS offering ORDER BY company, segment, offering LIMIT 4

example.question: Based on this graph, what business segments at Aurora Systems named Cloud Infrastructure serve IT professionals and sell through system integrators?
example.gold_cypher:
MATCH (c:Company)-[:HAS_SEGMENT]->(s:BusinessSegment)
WHERE s.company_name = c.name AND c.name IN $companies AND s.name IN $segments
MATCH (s)-[:SERVES]->(:CustomerType {name: $customer_type})
MATCH (s)-[:SELLS_THROUGH]->(:Channel {name: $channel})
RETURN DISTINCT c.name AS company, s.name AS segment ORDER BY company, segment
